changeSet.author: dev
changeSet.comment:
Add a deterministic global-salt lookup hash for API keys so the auth filter can resolve the owning user with a single indexed SELECT instead of iterating every active user and hashing against each one's per-user salt.
The legacy O(N) scan (a) scales linearly with active-user count, (b) spams the authentication rate limiter with false-positive "failed attempt" records on every non-matching user's identifier on every request — eventually locking out unrelated identifiers in Redis. Both go away once this column is populated and the filter is wired through the new path.
Existing users get NULL on day 1 and stay on the legacy path until they next rotate (eager fill via ApiKeyService.generateApiKey) or a successful auth triggers the lazy backfill in ApiKeyAuthenticationFilter — whichever comes first.
